claims: evaluate_reviewer_panel is conservative non-weighted set-membership aggregation
stdio.py:1306 couples cursor accept to panel accept, an AND-narrowing that cannot loosen existing blocks
reviewer-unavailable recovery preserved separately; panel does not mask recovery receipts
low_confidence_threshold defaults to 0.0 (permissive) and is config-tunable + plumbed through stdio/cli
5 panel tests are real public-boundary run_dual_agent_workflow tests with substantive assertions
confidence_criteria: panel decision recorded on new+legacy events+gate metadata
diff frozen at 497 ins/7 files matching execution gate
outcome.confidence_rationale: All P1-P5 + 6 user stories realized and corroborated by reading aggregator, end-to-end wiring (stdio.py:1290/1306/1327, agent_mailbox.py:379, config.py:137), and substantive non-vacuous public-boundary tests; diff frozen and matches execution gate. Held below 0.95 solely because pytest was not executed this round (operator-approval residual), so GREEN is by-inspection not by-run.

assumptions_to_verify: test runtime behavior matches by-inspection tracing of assertions to wiring keys
no hidden import/runtime error in stdio.py panel branch that inspection missed
contradictions_checked: Does the panel ever loosen an existing block? No — stdio.py:1306 makes accept conditional on panel==accept (AND-narrowing only).

Does reviewer-unavailable get masked by panel as quality revise? No — unavailable test asserts recovery receipts written + verdict_counted_as_accept=False while panel records missing; genuine-missing test asserts recovery absent.
Are tests vacuous? No — assertions reference final_gate_result['independent_reviewer_panel_decision'] populated only by new wiring.
missing_evidence: recorded pytest pass/fail output for the 7 tests
CI run artifact for the diff
critical_review.strongest_objection: pytest was not executed this round; all GREEN status is verified by code inspection of frozen source and test bodies rather than a recorded passing run.
critical_review.what_would_change_my_mind: An executed pytest run showing any of the 7 tests fail, or evidence the stdio.py:1306 coupling weakens an existing block path.

outcome.summary: outcome_review (final) gate: ACCEPT. Frozen tree (497 ins/7 files, matches execution gate + impl-plan hash 25b485e6) realizes all PRD promise contracts P1-P5 and 6 user stories. evaluate_reviewer_panel (reviewer_registry.py) is pure non-weighted set-membership: critical/important revise|deny hard-blocks, missing verdict never accepts, low-conf accept escalates, high-conf accept advances at permissive default 0.0. Wiring at stdio.py:1290/1306 AND-narrows accept to panel==accept (tightens, never loosens). Panel+per-reviewer inputs exported on new+legacy events. 5 real public-boundary tests + unavailable-augment are non-vacuous (assert on wiring-populated keys). Non-goals honored. Sole residual: pytest-not-run (operator approval).
